title: narcissus tazetta subsp. canariensis
intro: Narcissus tazetta subsp. canariensis, also known as the Canarian daffodil or Canarian narcissus, is a subspecies of the Narcissus tazetta plant, a type of bulbous perennial native to the Canary Islands. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve flatulence, as well as its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Narcissus tazetta subsp. canariensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are often used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
